name: Time Sensitive Letter (via VPP)
subject: Justice Sotomayor’s DIRE Warning >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
Our jaws are on the floor...
| _Justice Sotomayor just put the MAGA SCOTUS majority & Donald Trump to SHAME:_ [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/94930?t=6&akid=21526%2E878179%2E_IsoFx) **If a member of the Supreme Court — _who is supposed to be impartial_ — is sounding the alarm, _we should ALL be listening._**

Team, we’re so grateful for Sotomayor’s BRAVE leadership — but Republicans are FURIOUS with Sotomayor, and they’re _plotting revenge._

We need to show Supreme Court Justice Sotomayor that Democrats (especially Democrats in your state) have her back!

[**As one of the FEW protectors of our democracy left on the Supreme Court, we’re sending Sotomayor a letter thanking her for her incredible work. WE HAVE ONLY 18 SPOTS LEFT:**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/94930?t=7&akid=21526%2E878179%2E_IsoFx) | [**Donate $3 to sign the letter now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/94930?t=8&akid=21526%2E878179%2E_IsoFx)
---
TO: Justice Sonia Sotomayor
FROM: A proud Democrat
Subject: Thank you for taking a stand. **Dear Justice Sotomayor,

Thank you for standing up for our democracy against Donald Trump’s authoritarian agenda and blatant disregard of the Constitution.**

Your bravery continues to blow us away, time after time.

**As Trump tries to seize more and more control from Congress and the courts, your resilience is needed now more than ever.**

I’m sick and tired of the GOP letting him off scot-free — so I’m doing everything in my power to elect Democrats and ensure we appoint more amazing Supreme Court Justices like YOU in the near future.

[**I know you’re facing relentless attacks from Trump and his MAGA minions, so I’m signing this thank you letter and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to help Democrats keep fighting for justice »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/94930?t=9&akid=21526%2E878179%2E_IsoFx) [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/94930?t=10&akid=21526%2E878179%2E_IsoFx) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/94930?t=11&akid=21526%2E878179%2E_IsoFx)
---
**_X A grateful Democrat_**
—The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=21526%2E878179%2E_IsoFx). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=21526%2E878179%2E_IsoFx).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=21526%2E878179%2E_IsoFx) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=12&akid=21526%2E878179%2E_IsoFx). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=21526%2E878179%2E_IsoFx)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=21526%2E878179%2E_IsoFx)

name: David Hogg
subject: We just endorsed our first 2026 primary challenger
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Peter, this is exactly why we started Leaders We Deserve.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20251018-1&t=1&akid=2753%2E587540%2E1UwCRy)
Peter, this is exactly why we started Leaders We Deserve.
So we could endorse candidates like **Donavan McKinney** — and take on ineffective, corrupt incumbents like Shri Thanedar in the Democratic primaries.
[](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251018_1_mckinney.YKx7IL&refcode=em20251018-2753&amount=5&t=3&akid=2753%2E587540%2E1UwCRy)
Donavan is a young organizer, a fighter for Detroit, and someone who _actually_ delivers. He’s already helped secure:
✅ $600 million to replace lead pipes poisoning Michigan families
✅ $10 million for community violence intervention
✅ Millions more for public schools and community centers
Donavan’s written bills to stop monopoly utility companies from buying off politicians while Detroiters are left choosing between heat and groceries.
**Now he’s running for Congress — and he’s refusing corporate PAC money.**
Because he’s not here to play the game. He’s here to change it.
**[Will you split a $5 donation between Donovan McKinney and Leaders We Deserve today to help him take on the establishment and win?](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251018_1_mckinney.YKx7IL&refcode=em20251018-2753&amount=5&t=4&akid=2753%2E587540%2E1UwCRy)**
Shri Thanedar has spent years cozying up to special interests like AIPAC and using personal wealth to buy his seat. Meanwhile, people in Detroit are still struggling to afford rent, stay safe, and access basic services. People wonder why the Democratic party’s approval is so low, but when we have corrupt, ineffective representatives in our party it's no wonder voters aren’t excited to get behind our message.
Donavan is the opposite of that. He’s shown up for his community his entire life and he’s never stopped fighting for working people.
That’s the kind of leadership we need. And it’s exactly why we launched Leaders We Deserve in the first place: to support badass young progressives like Donavan who are taking on the status quo and building power from the ground up.
This is one of the most important primaries in the country. **We need to help Donavan win this race** and we’re counting on grassroots donors like you to make it possible.
**[Help elect a real leader to Congress. Donate $5 to Donavan McKinney and Leaders We Deserve today.](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251018_1_mckinney.YKx7IL&refcode=em20251018-2753&amount=5&t=5&akid=2753%2E587540%2E1UwCRy)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251018_1_mckinney.YKx7IL&amount=5&refcode=em20251018-2753&t=24&akid=2753%2E587540%2E1UwCRy)**
Thank you, Peter.
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20251018-1&t=25&refcode2=2753_587540_1UwCRy&akid=2753%2E587540%2E1UwCRy)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=26&akid=2753%2E587540%2E1UwCRy)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=27&akid=2753%2E587540%2E1UwCRy)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

name: Josh Harder
subject: Trump is not a king
body:
If you’re planning to join a protest today, thank you. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117536735/576806168/-1860252395?refcode=-header-&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYWUwN2VhZTEtM2ZhYy1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZWQ4YmE0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=TrsefCcdnrjn7mYbeXsNJtD9w_7n1Bt0hBjsuspkCJQ=) | Peter, Today, millions of Americans across the country are gathering to send one simple message to Donald Trump: _**You are not a king.**_ If you’re planning to join a protest today, thank you. It’s expected to shatter records as one of the largest demonstrations our country has ever seen! Peaceful, patriotic Americans are flooding the streets today because they love this country enough to speak out when it’s in danger. It gives me hope that we, the people, can stand up against Trump’s authoritarian power grab, his contempt for the Constitution, and his belief that he’s above the law. **We can’t allow Trump to turn our democracy into a dictatorship. |
Peter,
Over the past few weeks, the November 4th Pennsylvania Supreme Court election has gone thermonuclear and become the most important race in the nation.
The stakes of this race are unimaginably high. If Democrats lose here, MAGA can ensure we never win Pennsylvania and its 19 electoral college votes for the next decade and beyond.
Over the past few weeks Pennsylvania voters have been flooded with messaging pushed by organizations supported by ultra-MAGA billionaire Jeff Yass, the richest man in PA, that use progressive messaging (protect democracy, protect women, vote for term limits) and encourages them to vote no which is the GOP's ballot choice.
This has been the defining story of this race and polling has shown that this is confusing Democratic and Independent voters and leading some to inadvertently vote for MAGA.

Today Defend Our Courts just pulled off nothing short of a coup and strategically disrupted MAGA's plans, fundamentally altering the trajectory of this race.
Utilizing insider information leaked to us by a source within the Pennsylvania conservative printing industry, we just spent $150,000 to send a counter-mailer to 210,000 of the Democratic and Independent households who were targeted with MAGA's disinformation mailers.
Our message counters MAGA's deceptions by making it abundantly clear that the only way to protect abortion in Pennsylvania, a stance deeply popular amongst Democrats and Independents alike, is to vote yes (the Democratic ballot choice) on November 4th.

A month ago this race seemed all but lost for Democrats but because of you, and the hundreds of thousands of grassroots donors standing beside you, we have changed the calculus of this race and a victory for Democrats is within reach. Thank you.

subject: Together, we’re building something powerful
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20251018-1579-foote&t=1001&akid=1579%2E342411%2ExJYHxT ]Squad Victory Fund

Hey Peter,

The Squad Victory Fund isn’t here for corporate PACs, billionaire agendas,
or the same old status quo that puts profits before people.

We’re here for you — the working-class people across this country who are
ready for transformative change, and are willing to put in the work to get
there.

And that’s what makes this movement special: it’s powered by people like
you. That means our work is bigger than one election cycle — it’s about
building lasting power for our neighbors, our communities, and our future.

Together, we’ve built something powerful:

✅ We’re defending our progressive champions like AOC, Ayanna, Ilhan, and
Rashida that bring the voices of working-class people to the halls of
Congress every single day.

✅ We’re expanding our grassroots movement across the country to fight for
a future rooted in justice and equity.

✅ We’re standing up to the far-right’s extremism and corruption every
single day.

The Squad isn’t just here to play defense. We’re fighting on all fronts,
pushing for bold legislation on the issues that matter most, like Medicare
for All, climate action, student debt cancellation, affordable housing — a
people-first agenda that works for the many, not the wealthy few.

But this work doesn’t happen alone. We need you with us to keep the
momentum going.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20251018-movement.Z_pCen&refcode=em20251018-1579&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1579%2E342411%2ExJYHxT ]Can you chip in $3 today to keep our movement strong?

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20251018-movement.Z_pCen&refcode=em20251018-1579&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=1579%2E342411%2ExJYHxT ]Contribute ››

Let’s keep building — together.

In solidarity,

Squad Victory HQ

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1579%2E342411%2ExJYHxT

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

name: Powered by People
subject: Not good news in the Supreme Court
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE
body:
[1]Powered By People

Republicans are at it again—and this time, they’ve taken their fight all
the way to the Supreme Court.

After Republicans jammed through one of the most blatant partisan
gerrymanders in Texas history with Trump’s power grab, GOP lawmakers in
Louisiana are now trying to undo their own state’s maps—the ones that
finally gave minority voters a voice under the Voting Rights Act.

Louisiana Republicans are pushing the Court to ban any consideration of
race when drawing Congressional districts. If they get their way, it could
gut the Voting Rights Act and allow Republicans to redraw as many as 19
Democratic House seats to favor their party—including five in Texas.

Sound familiar? It’s the same playbook they’ve been running right here in
Texas: draw unfair maps, silence voters, and hold onto power at any cost.

We’ve seen this before—but at Powered by People, we know how to fight
back. We organize. We register new voters. We build power from the ground
up. And we don’t wait to act.

No matter how the Supreme Court rules, we need to be ready to win fair
representation the hard way—by showing up, talking to our neighbors,
registering voters on college campuses, and out-organizing their schemes
at every level of the ballot.

That’s where your support makes the difference. Every dollar we raise
powers voter registration and our organizing in the communities most
impacted by these attacks on fair representation.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251018.gPHJLs&refcode=ema20251018-240411&t=1002&akid=240411%2E444211%2E8OwudS ]Will you chip in $3 to power our grassroots organizing
efforts and make sure we’re ready for whatever comes next? Because no
matter how they try to rewrite the rules and rig the maps, people power
wins when we organize.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251018.gPHJLs&refcode=ema20251018-240411&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=240411%2E444211%2E8OwudS ]DONATE $3

Thank you,

Powered by People

PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE

Charlie Kirk’s Mission Continues
Help build the RED WALL—carry the torch for Charlie in key battleground states.
| [ ](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=f09b4c7429544d7cb586fa78c3ad1177&_e=xSZ0-abqY0pzLi1rPWFyrnrzQ-NNCpbMHxfIPqs7VHcoeqliu6WFWMwNHL_kdbqXo-OE8-1fB7gDPsYr8li3TJBAiFlZUD5a5vlSaZpnpTY0nGZICp2XSGxmXbzbxTCi-a1wt8kjxdL0Peci9Pay9-tcxPyMg8x7v7Q_YvcDsVxXat4ISSEkI7Xgmz_zpsG8oQAJOjUGbQEncT_29VznFBn4XlbpEYty5C5fDyLcyRYHir3XXJhyQRGhry2V1y-EfdTrIOOAPkaWTXS56qvgWHhCTYGsE5ZfyPee4GY2LwR6RRSePYu9CGeZe5rF_ZQcd_j-bgHoFiV7cqblfYhn3fK_So5NcyjeqJ6yJKrA54ksmoQnQ-rc3Uk9XXYTsf-v5xcfc4pAJ-jSvVcf5zkL1Bqvt_dF49O0089prg5YazCnuQpXEcBEXX23VFGSx1vsoHxq6NG2U-1qI4XdCfzbOkzcFGRFu35BQnRylYnl9hO22mzgGONdvmS4_Se0Cw05j8dKK4wd5GWy1Kq3Yu5mgSwtw_3g4B55u1CAKuMC9lX0VpthEK5J3Azg_4x4sY1GRT0IgVnR5FSqBbARkQTNTg%3D%3D)

Patriot,

When I first met Charlie Kirk, it was instantly clear to me that he was destined to do great things.

We became fast friends.

---
[ ](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=f09b4c7429544d7cb586fa78c3ad1177&_e=xSZ0-abqY0pzLi1rPWFyrnrzQ-NNCpbMHxfIPqs7VHcoeqliu6WFWMwNHL_kdbqXo-OE8-1fB7gDPsYr8li3TJBAiFlZUD5a5vlSaZpnpTY0nGZICp2XSGxmXbzbxTCi-a1wt8kjxdL0Peci9Pay992sVRj5FBOvGZ5hVqNpMcJo02cQPAAAi7ovIZOLLzGwr2nZcGGDQ9hsajsVBiSCBedSnuq40XpEpAIbRNSa7gEQusQO7FQB2MJWOM5tvnnE7bh19Ui1ZWutl7sKbZp5uP96JDd0dqcoU-Sg6iZMXPdstxgBl3pJanBsQYYh_6mBdQYTVQ6tqvmt2fBYxdg0oeLU4wTpIYn6Md5vMAVKw283I8OMX2sWnhlv84LNGjWl-6RU1kE9hst_0EwkenhO6Sh9jjm_hIp7rvBkWsZIvnEG_00Wt4HuiisTtJRJJqcFIDFJKMBFBG18wh9lLCa-G4FiZJaHWXdv6qCwasNhqKQD2-tveAx0JYH1hv4PtrT_GdmE_2T_v790Ie4_QTamGzF2MAldBlgq5GrrAK6xkiDIn3347deZVO5peTghD6knIDI9UyJJMGZFVoGbikk4Qw%3D%3D)
---

Turning Point USA was still in its infancy, but there was no doubt in my mind that, with Charlie's indomitable spirit and vision, Turning Point would transform the conservative movement and the country as a whole.

Fast forward 11 years, and the political landscape has, indeed, changed, thanks to Charlie's bold leadership.

In 2024, Charlie won the youth vote for President Trump... an unprecedented accomplishment.

**It 's been one month since Charlie Kirk's assassination.**

And, while no one can truly replace him, I know -- without a shadow of a doubt -- that he would want us to continue the fight for the heart and soul of America in his stead.

And that's exactly what I plan to do... win for Charlie.

At Turning Point PAC, we have not lost sight of **Charlie 's big picture vision to Build the RED WALL**, starting with state and local races in Arizona, New Hampshire, and Nevada, the three battleground states where _conservatives outnumber Democrats_.

By focusing on these states, we can build an impenetrable Red Wall that will destroy the Democrats' plans and pave the way to victory for America First candidates in 2026, 2028, and beyond.

Every seat won -- up and down the ballot -- is a victory that will reap long-term dividends.

**So, today I ask:** _Will you help me carry the torch for Charlie?_

[ Please donate whatever you can generously afford today to WIN in key battleground states. We will stand FEARLESS, like Charlie did, in defense of what we know is RIGHT! >> ](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=f09b4c7429544d7cb586fa78c3ad1177&_e=xSZ0-abqY0pzLi1rPWFyrnrzQ-NNCpbMHxfIPqs7VHcoeqliu6WFWMwNHL_kdbqXo-OE8-1fB7gDPsYr8li3TJBAiFlZUD5a5vlSaZpnpTY0nGZICp2XSGxmXbzbxTCi-a1wt8kjxdL0Peci9Pay9zWWdQuOB--xto5UjfvZqFCrU2bGN_Nzokd9s8Zj1FsChk5xzFzoMhmNbrX4Ps0UiK5uyhXm9ILdnTFTg2M2aqaywm9PioIEwgoBxgWnjanNN7UsJNyRTJ31H55dygUgJUTr_04TJ34FwqUoHX9SraxG6OKvnYDHoDHtHTH3dUAc8Wa97TMoaCuFzrBc_oS_LA7Y_YGwCDj68swkEHBNqbL2qVAA58hQ-Pazq1uSU2b3c_uNuZ67L6AIKcuiKQ38R76ySXvnSH1jyg3rKoyTlIxQH4OlfjISPgsQywCRWKcBSS7_1_STaQQSIwtF-9FjZ7IyuoCiGO9a_bC3DrYbfpvygeJBGyejMfpmsNgKSFHG76UpTU8Q8miu-AwWTI5LOtLKtPWJis3S0VI80SHyXAMOOIWbS5Rcw3SnaOeNqOULWHVWJ3j7uyKIb7k63aVtiA3tpdmrbA24Ud2SCtfmXNU%3D)

---

For Charlie.

God Bless,
---

**Tyler Bowyer**
Executive Director
Turning Point PAC

Non-Contribution Account (For TTPAC, Inc.'s Expenditures Other Than Direct Contributions to Federal Committees)

As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee's name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information.

Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law.

Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account are not tax-deductible for federal income tax purposes.

Paid for by Turning Point PAC, Inc. (www.tppac.com) and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
---

Phoenix, AZ 85044

# [The Green Left Crippled America While Empowering China For 25 Years](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4720068&post_id=176073288&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc2MDczMjg4LCJpYXQiOjE3NjA4MDcxNjIsImV4cCI6MTc2MzM5OTE2MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQ3MjAwNjgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.32VuBfRLdaIr_EPZIR8QfDED1p5_CwFVYLaNKRmAda4)
### The Hidden Cost of Al Gore's Green Virtue Is The Real Inconvenient Truth
| | [The Capitalist](https://substack.com/@capitalistmag)
---
| Oct 18
---
| [](https://substack.com/@capitalistmag)

| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4720068&post_id=176073288&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc2MDczMjg4LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2MDgwNzE2MiwiZXhwIjoxNzYzMzk5MTYyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDcyMDA2OCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.bIcW8csE2fUGSzsfCIA_RpRGCOZETLDc9xzbI6iXhJw&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

* * *
* * *
### The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication
### Reject Corporate Left Wing Journalism
[Support What We Do](https://substack.com/redirect/392ff93f-3992-44df-b03a-9d9f1a99dd81?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
### _**" It's scandalous that we don't have a rare earth strategic reserve... We let China monopolize 90 percent of refining rare earth materials. **_
### _**Where were we, realizing the importance of these? "**_
_**-[Jeremy Siegel, Professor Emeritus of Finance, University of Pennsylvania - CNBC](https://substack.com/redirect/91f23cc0-ad04-4414-9673-d63e91bc117a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/91f23cc0-ad04-4414-9673-d63e91bc117a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The answer it turns out is remarkably simple.
We listened to Al Gore and we realize now, **that was a mistake.**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/689f1111-5df3-401e-a9af-0ceef3b19ec3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In the shadow of California's Mojave Desert, the Mountain Pass Mine once hummed as the beating heart of America's rare-earth empire. Discovered in 1949 amid uranium hunts gone awry, its veins of bastnasite ore yielded the esoteric metals that would power the postwar technological boom--from color televisions to missile guidance systems.
By the 1980s, this open-pit scar in the earth supplied nearly 70 percent of the world's rare earths. Then, in 2002, after a toxic spill of radioactive wastewater tainted nearby streams, the mine shuttered.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/04530ff4-f3ad-46fb-9b81-d4cde8617c88?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Environmental regulators descended, Chinese competitors, more than happy to carry the burden, flooded the market with cheap alternatives, and what followed was a quarter-century of American complacency and environmental virtue signaling.
Today, as electric vehicles and AI super computers chips demand ever more of these minerals, the irony stings: The green revolution Al Gore helped ignite has left the U.S. perilously dependent on the very nation it seeks to stay ahead of.
* * *

# [Is Your News Lying to You? ](https://substack.com/redirect/9a139968-0e80-4158-b14b-d29172a94bee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

[Download the Free App](https://substack.com/redirect/9a139968-0e80-4158-b14b-d29172a94bee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
**[Rare earths](https://substack.com/redirect/b2d42ba8-6c94-4247-baa4-d097981fa84f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** --a misnomer for 17 chemically similar elements like neodymium, dysprosium, and yttrium--are not geological rarities; in fact they're more abundant than copper. Yet their extraction and refinement are fiendishly complex, laced with toxic acids and radioactive byproducts. These metals are the invisible sinews of modern life: neodymium magnets propel EV motors and wind turbine blades; europium lights up smartphone screens; terbium sharpens fighter-jet lasers. Without them, the net-zero dream--Gore's clarion call since his **[2006 film An Inconvenient Truth](https://substack.com/redirect/301b197f-dbed-49db-b1da-62b51b378508?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** --crumbles. **[According to the International Energy Agency](https://substack.com/redirect/347287ed-cea1-4c23-9de5-b2dfff4bdafe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , rare earths will see demand surge 40-fold by 2040 to fuel clean energy transitions.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a6c552a6-84c4-4490-bbe2-a8e4d78993fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
But the American environmental Left's zealous pursuit of "Green Virtue" above all else, outsourced this vital national life blood (and others - Oil drilling, refining, robotics, chip production) to Beijing (among others - Middle East, Africa, Asia.) Where lax global standards have turned an American economic monopoly into dangerous anti-American geopolitical leverage. The bill for that complacency is now due, and it is going to have to be paid, and its going to be expensive.
The seeds of this vulnerability were sown in the late 1990s, as Al Gore, then Vice-President, championed the Kyoto Protocol and a global shift away from fossil fuels. His vision, amplified by his book "Earth in the Balance" (1992), framed environmentalism as a moral imperative, prioritizing emission cuts and environmental action over resource security. Idealism clashed with reality, and reality lost.
* * *
### The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication
### Reject Corporate Left Wing Journalism
[Support What We Do](https://substack.com/redirect/392ff93f-3992-44df-b03a-9d9f1a99dd81?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
Rare-earth mining, like fossil fuel extraction, invited scrutiny from the environmental left--groups like the Sierra Club, which Gore's rhetoric emboldened. The result? A cascade of stalled projects that handed China 90 percent of global rare earth refining capacity.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/186b1a04-3f89-414d-929b-4f79a3be7213?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**[Consider Mountain Pass itself](https://substack.com/redirect/186b1a04-3f89-414d-929b-4f79a3be7213?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. Intended to produce cerium, lanthanum, and neodymium oxides for magnets and catalysts, the mine--operated first by Unocal, then Molycorp--closed in 2002 amid environmental fines and spills that leaked heavy metals into the desert aquifer.
Revived in 2010 with $500 million in investments, Molycorp's expansion faltered against Chinese price dumping. By 2015, **[the firm filed for bankruptcy,](https://substack.com/redirect/3aaccac9-532d-4eeb-aaa8-4c154d64f101?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** saddled with $1.7 billion in debt and unable to compete in a market flooded by Beijing's cheap (state-subsidized) output. The human toll was immediate: hundreds of jobs lost in a rural economy already battered by deindustrialization.
Farther north, in Alaska's Bristol Bay watershed, the Pebble Mine promised a different bounty--not rare earths per se, but copper, gold, and molybdenum laced with rhenium, a rare-earth analog critical for superalloys in jet engines. Envisioned as one of the world's largest undeveloped deposits, Pebble could have yielded 6.5 billion pounds of copper over decades, bolstering U.S. supplies for EV batteries and defense tech. Yet the EPA **[vetoed its permit](https://substack.com/redirect/7e84cd95-bb3d-4366-878f-7cc8e278224f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** under Clean Water Act Section 404(c), citing risks to the world's largest sockeye salmon fishery.
Environmentalists hailed the decision, but it epitomized the "out of sight, out of mind" ethos: Block domestic extraction, ignore the future fallout. This pattern of obstruction echoes a broader shortsightedness by the environmental left. **[Decades of opposition to nuclear power](https://substack.com/redirect/6d5462cf-0220-4b62-a661-813480386676?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** --fueled by concerns over incidents like Three Mile Island (1979) and Chernobyl (1986)--halted reactor construction, leaving the U.S. with aging plants, stagnant capacity and a fantastical promise that wind and solar would cover all future needs. Then AI was invented.
**[Goldman Sachs warns](https://substack.com/redirect/1aa8b274-86cc-420a-a60a-3d2be383d9b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that AI alone could add 160 percent to power needs by 2030, straining grids built for a pre-AI past. As AI data centers devour electricity--**[projected to drive 20 percent of U.S. demand growth by 2030](https://substack.com/redirect/6b59088f-39c2-41e8-9ec7-3b0df2a0b823?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** --the nation faces blackouts, soaring bills, **[a lot of dead birds,](https://substack.com/redirect/2992793b-6dca-449f-8f9b-21deac2e7c21?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and a timeframe to correct this egregious error that is in all likelihood measured in decades.
The rare earth peril America faces demands a strategic reserve, akin to the one born from the 1973 oil crisis. (Hell, even **[Canada has a maple syrup reserve](https://substack.com/redirect/898d746c-93ec-453d-a93f-290125a6b2f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to preserve its most vital nation resource.) Established in 1975 under the Energy Policy and Conservation Act, the **[Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR)](https://substack.com/redirect/91ebb8d5-120d-4ebc-9359-2da1810b9e5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** stockpiles 714 million barrels in Gulf Coast caverns to blunt supply shocks.
The SPR cushioned the nation through hurricanes and embargoes, proving that foresight fortifies sovereignty. Rare earths warrant the same treatment: a buffered stockpile to weather Beijing's whims, which was proved when in 2010 China **[halted exports](https://substack.com/redirect/d5785731-a3bc-40fc-af2c-2e908482fa75?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to Japan, spiking prices 500 percent.
Enter Donald Trump, whose second term echoes this logic with urgency. In March 2025, he signed an executive order to **["make the U.S. the leading producer"](https://substack.com/redirect/8a4da7c1-e948-499c-8615-581161913dd8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/8a4da7c1-e948-499c-8615-581161913dd8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)of critical minerals, streamlining permits and pledging billions in investments.
The Pentagon's July stake in MP Materials--converting grants to equity in Mountain Pass--**[provides a blueprint of how this solution will have to be implemented.](https://substack.com/redirect/5ae15518-90a3-4adf-a91a-af42b213489a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** The cheapest price is no longer the sole concern. National security and domestic production trumps lower prices and given **[JP Morgan CEO Jamie Dimon's latest comments](https://substack.com/redirect/ef62ea15-941a-4eeb-b6c6-2066dfc74577?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** it appears that Wall Street agrees. The Pentagon's price floor-price guarantees will shield miners from Chinese dumping and make investment in America's resources viable once again and will likely be used as the model going forward.
By unlocking domestic deposits--from **[Texas's Round Top](https://substack.com/redirect/2a7a4553-e3fd-4141-a9da-219045775704?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to **[Wyoming's Bear Lodge](https://substack.com/redirect/f43a866a-c107-47d8-b436-4666c7fa82a6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**--the policy aims to reassert American supremacy on a world map that has tilted toward Beijing under previous administrations, while also fostering alliances with Australia and Vietnam to diversify supply chains and blunt China's adventurism.
Yet this reckoning exposes a deeper fracture in the green movement. Gore's net-zero moralizing crusade, morphed into a selective puritanism: decry pollution at home, avert eyes abroad. It crushed U.S. industries while inflating global inequities, leaving America chasing its tail in the AI race and beyond.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6494cb98-0901-49d5-8ac5-96834161b631?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Another irony? Green policies crushed homegrown energy and resource production while still craving its virtue signaling fruits--smart thermostats, solar panels and EVs--which are now largely built and sourced abroad.
Nowhere is the human cost of this more grotesque than in Africa, where outsourced demand breeds horror. In the Democratic Republic of Congo, **[artisanal cobalt mines](https://substack.com/redirect/40841548-343c-418f-8adf-48c31b9a1cac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** --vital for rare-earth-adjacent batteries--employ 40,000 children in pits where cave-ins claim lives weekly and deforested hillsides leach acids into rivers.
Workers, **[including boys as young as six,](https://substack.com/redirect/194614e2-587b-4b49-bb41-cafcd9a22632?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** hack ore from the ground with picks for $2 a day amid toxic dust and sexual exploitation in some 19th century nightmare come to life.
**[U.S. firms like Apple and Tesla,](https://substack.com/redirect/a4d83162-23a4-452e-bfed-4a69760829dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** champions of net zero, trace their supply chains to these hellscapes, proving that domestic regulations at home did not prevent unchecked environmental devastation and human suffering, they simply moved it to a place that most Americans will never see it or hear about it.
As the Age of AI dawns, with its data centers and their thirst for power and EVs and their appetite for batteries, the lingering question is becoming more urgent: Can we forge a sustainability that safeguards both planet and power, or will "Green Virtue's" blind spots doom us to foreign dependency?
America has a complicated past with environmentalism, from the earliest days of the Westward Expansion the pursuit of America's natural resource wealth has scarred the land, but we have come a long way as a nation since the Age of the Robber Barons.
This is not a call for unfettered pillaging of resources with not a care paid to the consequences. There should be worker safety regulations, and sensible environmental regulations, and stiff consequences for unsafe practices that violate any or all of those. But there also has to be an acceptance of reality, that if America is to compete in this new age, there will be accidents, there will be casualties. We should do nothing but our absolute best to avoid them and mitigate them when they happen but the answer cannot be to do nothing in the name of environmental zealotry - and then shift the problem out the back door on to the shoulders of a six year old Congolese child miner who we don't know and don't have to look in the face of in the name of morally bankrupt "Green Virtue."
The desert winds at Mountain Pass whisper a warning--reclamation is possible, but only if we mine our own resolve.
* * *
* * *

The Hidden Cost of Al Gore's Green Virtue Is The Real Inconvenient Truth | | | Oct 18 | | | | | | | | | | | | The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication Reject Corporate Left Wing Journalism " It's scandalous that we don't have a rare earth strategic reserve... We let China monopolize 90 percent of refining rare earth materials. Where were we, realizing the importance of these? " - The answer it turns out is remarkably simple. We listened to Al Gore and we realize now, that was a mistake. | | ---|---|--- In the shadow of California's Mojave Desert, the Mountain Pass Mine once hummed as the beating heart of America's rare-earth empire. Discovered in 1949 amid uranium hunts gone awry, its veins of bastnasite ore yielded the esoteric metals that would power the postwar technological boom--from color televisions to missile guidance systems. By the 1980s, this open-pit scar in the earth supplied nearly 70 percent of the world's rare earths. Then, in 2002, after a toxic spill of radioactive wastewater tainted nearby streams, the mine shuttered. | | ---|---|--- Environmental regulators descended, Chinese competitors, more than happy to carry the burden, flooded the market with cheap alternatives, and what followed was a quarter-century of American complacency and environmental virtue signaling. Today, as electric vehicles and AI super computers chips demand ever more of these minerals, the irony stings: The green revolution Al Gore helped ignite has left the U.S. perilously dependent on the very nation it seeks to stay ahead of. | | ---|---|--- In today's media world, misinformation spreads faster than markets can react. One minute you're reading about 401(k) changes, the next you're wondering if China's rare earth metals exports are really the key to America's future, or just another hype cycle. Freespoke helps you separate truth from misinformation. With one tap, you'll see the full picture with what experts agree on, where they differ, and what's being overlooked. It even surfaces answers from publishers you trust, like The Capitalist, so you can know what's reliable. It's anonymous, clean, and uncensored. No tracking. No data harvesting. Just truthful information. Whether you're fact-checking a viral investing claim, researching energy trends, or trying to understand what's really happening with your retirement, Freespoke helps you ask smarter questions and get full truth answers. Premium gives you ad-free browsing (no more creepy Big Tech trackers), unlimited AI summaries that break down complex financial question in seconds, and searchable podcast transcripts that pull truth straight from the experts. If you want the truth, download the free app today and see what's really going on. --a misnomer for 17 chemically similar elements like neodymium, dysprosium, and yttrium--are not geological rarities; in fact they're more abundant than copper. Yet their extraction and refinement are fiendishly complex, laced with toxic acids and radioactive byproducts. These metals are the invisible sinews of modern life: neodymium magnets propel EV motors and wind turbine blades; europium lights up smartphone screens; terbium sharpens fighter-jet lasers. Without them, the net-zero dream--Gore's clarion call since his --crumbles. , rare earths will see demand surge 40-fold by 2040 to fuel clean energy transitions. | | ---|---|--- But the American environmental Left's zealous pursuit of "Green Virtue" above all else, outsourced this vital national life blood (and others - Oil drilling, refining, robotics, chip production) to Beijing (among others - Middle East, Africa, Asia.) Where lax global standards have turned an American economic monopoly into dangerous anti-American geopolitical leverage. The bill for that complacency is now due, and it is going to have to be paid, and its going to be expensive. The seeds of this vulnerability were sown in the late 1990s, as Al Gore, then Vice-President, championed the Kyoto Protocol and a global shift away from fossil fuels. His vision, amplified by his book "Earth in the Balance" (1992), framed environmentalism as a moral imperative, prioritizing emission cuts and environmental action over resource security. Idealism clashed with reality, and reality lost. The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication Reject Corporate Left Wing Journalism Rare-earth mining, like fossil fuel extraction, invited scrutiny from the environmental left--groups like the Sierra Club, which Gore's rhetoric emboldened. The result? A cascade of stalled projects that handed China 90 percent of global rare earth refining capacity. | | ---|---|--- . Intended to produce cerium, lanthanum, and neodymium oxides for magnets and catalysts, the mine--operated first by Unocal, then Molycorp--closed in 2002 amid environmental fines and spills that leaked heavy metals into the desert aquifer. Revived in 2010 with $500 million in investments, Molycorp's expansion faltered against Chinese price dumping. By 2015, saddled with $1.7 billion in debt and unable to compete in a market flooded by Beijing's cheap (state-subsidized) output. The human toll was immediate: hundreds of jobs lost in a rural economy already battered by deindustrialization. Farther north, in Alaska's Bristol Bay watershed, the Pebble Mine promised a different bounty--not rare earths per se, but copper, gold, and molybdenum laced with rhenium, a rare-earth analog critical for superalloys in jet engines. Envisioned as one of the world's largest undeveloped deposits, Pebble could have yielded 6.5 billion pounds of copper over decades, bolstering U.S. supplies for EV batteries and defense tech. Yet the EPA under Clean Water Act Section 404(c), citing risks to the world's largest sockeye salmon fishery. Environmentalists hailed the decision, but it epitomized the "out of sight, out of mind" ethos: Block domestic extraction, ignore the future fallout. This pattern of obstruction echoes a broader shortsightedness by the environmental left. --fueled by concerns over incidents like Three Mile Island (1979) and Chernobyl (1986)--halted reactor construction, leaving the U.S. with aging plants, stagnant capacity and a fantastical promise that wind and solar would cover all future needs. Then AI was invented. that AI alone could add 160 percent to power needs by 2030, straining grids built for a pre-AI past. As AI data centers devour electricity-- --the nation faces blackouts, soaring bills, and a timeframe to correct this egregious error that is in all likelihood measured in decades. The rare earth peril America faces demands a strategic reserve, akin to the one born from the 1973 oil crisis. (Hell, even to preserve its most vital nation resource.) Established in 1975 under the Energy Policy and Conservation Act, the stockpiles 714 million barrels in Gulf Coast caverns to blunt supply shocks. The SPR cushioned the nation through hurricanes and embargoes, proving that foresight fortifies sovereignty. Rare earths warrant the same treatment: a buffered stockpile to weather Beijing's whims, which was proved when in 2010 China to Japan, spiking prices 500 percent. Enter Donald Trump, whose second term echoes this logic with urgency. In March 2025, he signed an executive order to critical minerals, streamlining permits and pledging billions in investments. The Pentagon's July stake in MP Materials--converting grants to equity in Mountain Pass-- The cheapest price is no longer the sole concern. National security and domestic production trumps lower prices and given it appears that Wall Street agrees. The Pentagon's price floor-price guarantees will shield miners from Chinese dumping and make investment in America's resources viable once again and will likely be used as the model going forward. By unlocking domestic deposits--from to policy aims to reassert American supremacy on a world map that has tilted toward Beijing under previous administrations, while also fostering alliances with Australia and Vietnam to diversify supply chains and blunt China's adventurism. Yet this reckoning exposes a deeper fracture in the green movement. Gore's net-zero moralizing crusade, morphed into a selective puritanism: decry pollution at home, avert eyes abroad. It crushed U.S. industries while inflating global inequities, leaving America chasing its tail in the AI race and beyond. | | ---|---|--- Another irony? Green policies crushed homegrown energy and resource production while still craving its virtue signaling fruits--smart thermostats, solar panels and EVs--which are now largely built and sourced abroad. subject: Trump said workers “don’t deserve backpay”

|

[➤➤ **Trump says federal workers may not get back pay**](font-size:18px;) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117538374/576810988/1409848552?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMjEzOWJlMi00M2FjLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YuY5LEZY5ZfBNdp_3KVM-C3L-6apQJqv_agUiTBfs-A=&emci=54fe9cda-86ab-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=22139be2-43ac-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=26828920)
* * *
This is cruel, friend. Last week, the Trump administration announced that furloughed federal workers will not be guaranteed back pay. Worse? Trump himself told reporters those workers “don’t deserve back pay.” These are the very people who kept our government afloat during crises — airport security agents, food inspectors, Social Security staff, park rangers, and so many others who showed up while paychecks were frozen. **And now Trump’s saying they’re not worth it.** It’s outrageous. It’s cruel. And it’s deliberate. This is all part of a broader strategy: use fear, economic pressure, and institutional chaos to consolidate power, while gutting trust in public service while he’s at it. Democrats in office are fighting back. We’re supporting legal efforts to enforce existing worker protections, hold this administration accountable, and stop this precedent from taking root. [**We’re sounding the alarm. Add your name to demand that federal workers be paid:**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117538375/576810989/1409848552?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMjEzOWJlMi00M2FjLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YuY5LEZY5ZfBNdp_3KVM-C3L-6apQJqv_agUiTBfs-A=&emci=54fe9cda-86ab-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=22139be2-43ac-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=26828920) | [Add your name](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117538376/576810990/1409848552?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMjEzOWJlMi00M2FjLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YuY5LEZY5ZfBNdp_3KVM-C3L-6apQJqv_agUiTBfs-A=&emci=54fe9cda-86ab-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=22139be2-43ac-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=26828920)

name: Trisha Calvarese
subject: No Kings: Boebert calls for theocratic monarchy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
That's exactly what Boebert and Trump want — a Christian nation ruled by King Trump and enforced by MAGA extremists.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001dOiHMyLu1aLGcVxVsq_tyXXCGC0aOiUXlzMKGonbOVB_0B7Q_zvHEnznvysHW1DTODV8v2cdiVBKOSbyIf3qeEl_gqQJ_AVrg4vD5AS13dLEkDeYEQd4EXXPZxJu98wSizIeXI1kCU4c4wleiVECgUW0eaPpLwBTQTPo5i4sygyoYiKiwUZrIwtTtISmWijLpsuvmmfDMjDZe-gb7g1Zkck0SE4kolqO&c=FSEGkMpJT74PHNE-5vxNMj1Psz_leJ9ew9HFDtBlhsUwMiGajsJ-6Q==&ch=V5KeRSfQYhgxgzjG1HvNrAayA03yyMdPN_4lAHbVgdraE3LIeI3PVA==)
---
|

Peter,
Right now, millions of Americans are taking to the streets for No Kings Day -- a national day of protest declaring that this country belongs to the people, not to Donald Trump or any would-be king.
I joined Coloradans marching here in our state today, shoulder to shoulder with families, workers, and students -- all united by one message: America will never kneel to a dictator.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001dOiHMyLu1aLGcVxVsq_tyXXCGC0aOiUXlzMKGonbOVB_0B7Q_zvHEnznvysHW1DTODV8v2cdiVBKOSbyIf3qeEl_gqQJ_AVrg4vD5AS13dLEkDeYEQd4EXXPZxJu98wSizIeXI1kCU4c4wleiVECgUW0eaPpLwBTQTPo5i4sygyoYiKiwUZrIwtTtISmWijLpsuvmmfDMjDG-L7_jTg2V3KjSFi2TIud&c=FSEGkMpJT74PHNE-5vxNMj1Psz_leJ9ew9HFDtBlhsUwMiGajsJ-6Q==&ch=V5KeRSfQYhgxgzjG1HvNrAayA03yyMdPN_4lAHbVgdraE3LIeI3PVA==)
---
In the theme of No Kings, I can't help but think of something Lauren Boebert once said: she's "tired of the separation between church and state."
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001dOiHMyLu1aLGcVxVsq_tyXXCGC0aOiUXlzMKGonbOVB_0B7Q_zvHEnznvysHW1DTODV8v2cdiVBKOSbyIf3qeEl_gqQJ_AVrg4vD5AS13dLEkDeYEQd4EXXPZxJu98wSizIeXI1kCU4c4wleiVECgUW0eaPpLwBTQTPo5i4sygyoYiKiwUZrIwtTtISmWijLpsuvmmfDMjD5dwjpT4xPPmq-RzPPZ6Qs&c=FSEGkMpJT74PHNE-5vxNMj1Psz_leJ9ew9HFDtBlhsUwMiGajsJ-6Q==&ch=V5KeRSfQYhgxgzjG1HvNrAayA03yyMdPN_4lAHbVgdraE3LIeI3PVA==)
---
That's what this movement is fighting against. America was founded in opposition to the dictates of any king, crown, or church. But that's exactly what Boebert and Trump want -- a Christian nation ruled by King Trump and enforced by MAGA extremists.
We're not bowing. Because America is a democracy, and we choose to keep it. Every single protest, every door knocked, every dollar raised helps ensure Boebert's brand of extremism never wins another election in Colorado. clean_body: That's exactly what Boebert and Trump want — a Christian nation ruled by King Trump and enforced by MAGA extremists. | | | | | == | Peter, Right now, millions of Americans are taking to the streets for No Kings Day -- a national day of protest declaring that this country belongs to the people, not to Donald Trump or any would-be king. I joined Coloradans marching here in our state today, shoulder to shoulder with families, workers, and students -- all united by one message: America will never kneel to a dictator. | == In the theme of No Kings, I can't help but think of something Lauren Boebert once said: she's "tired of the separation between church and state." | == That's what this movement is fighting against. America was founded in opposition to the dictates of any king, crown, or church. But that's exactly what Boebert and Trump want -- a Christian nation ruled by King Trump and enforced by MAGA extremists. We're not bowing. Because America is a democracy, and we choose to keep it. Did you see our email about the most recent attack on voting rights, Peter? Republicans aren’t just trying to undermine our voting rights — **they’re laying the groundwork to strip away the laws that give us those rights.** This isn’t a coincidence. It’s part of a coordinated strategy to control every lever of our democracy. From who can vote, to how votes are counted, to who oversees the process. We can’t afford for this to happen. If they’re mobilizing to dismantle voting rights from the inside out, **then we have to mobilize just as hard to protect them.** **Georgia is the front line.** We need to organize, defend our elections, and make sure every voter can cast a ballot that’s counted. **\---------- Forwarded message from Georgia Dems ----------**
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117539501/576815262/-14960666?amount=10&source=SBD_DPG_EM_FR_101825_SCOTUSVRAFWD_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZGRkNjY0YS00OGFjLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KKDsJ9tZ9qacMbxNNMGhPwKUkx1M5uUJ0doxRfeao2Q=)
Peter — this is **serious.**
At hearings on Wednesday, the conservative-controlled Supreme Court showed strong support for _gutting_ the Voting Rights Act of 1965, which was designed to protect people from being denied or restricted from voting based on race, color, or language.
This is part of a much bigger plan. **Since taking control, Republicans have:**
* Tried to kill mail-in voting
* Rammed through extreme gerrymandering
* Targeted election officials
* Undermined trust in our democracy at every turn

Now they’re coming for the very laws that our own John Lewis fought for during the Civil Rights movement — so it’s time for us to get into good trouble.
We’re not sitting back. We’re organizing across Georgia to protect voting rights for everyone, fight voter suppression, and hold the line for democracy. **But we can’t do it alone.**

clean_body: You need to know what they’re doing now. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | = Did you see our email about the most recent attack on voting rights, Peter? Republicans aren’t just trying to undermine our voting rights — they’re laying the groundwork to strip away the laws that give us those rights. This isn’t a coincidence. It’s part of a coordinated strategy to control every lever of our democracy. From who can vote, to how votes are counted, to who oversees the process. We can’t afford for this to happen. If they’re mobilizing to dismantle voting rights from the inside out, then we have to mobilize just as hard to protect them. Georgia is the front line. We need to organize, defend our elections, and make sure every voter can cast a ballot that’s counted. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = | = | = | = | = \---------- Forwarded message from Georgia Dems ---------- = Peter — this is serious. At hearings on Wednesday, the conservative-controlled Supreme Court showed strong support for gutting the Voting Rights Act of 1965, which was designed to protect people from being denied or restricted from voting based on race, color, or language. This is part of a much bigger plan. Since taking control, Republicans have: Tried to kill mail-in voting Rammed through extreme gerrymandering Targeted election officials Undermined trust in our democracy at every turn Now they’re coming for the very laws that our own John Lewis fought for during the Civil Rights movement — so it’s time for us to get into good trouble. name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Before our next deadline
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
This won't be an easy race, but I'm used to overcoming the odds.
Hi, it's Abigail Spanberger, Democratic nominee for Governor of Virginia. I'm respectfully asking: **[Will you split a $5 contribution right now between my campaign and Deb Haaland, before our next deadline?](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/807?c0=em_ms_dh_20251018_1_spanberger.Hx0FJ-&amount=5&refcode=em20251018-5129&t=1&akid=5129%2E400544%2E6XWCWO)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/807?c0=em_ms_dh_20251018_1_spanberger.Hx0FJ-&amount=5&refcode=em20251018-5129&t=2&akid=5129%2E400544%2E6XWCWO)**
As the only Democrat who can flip a Governor's seat blue this year, I want to set an example for the nation.
That's why we've knocked on over 700,000 doors so far. It's why we've launched plans to make life more affordable and strengthen our schools. And it's why we've committed to building the largest grassroots campaign in our Commonwealth's history.
This won't be an easy race, but I'm used to overcoming the odds.
Back in 2018, I ran for Congress in a district that Republicans had controlled for 50 years. People told me that I shouldn't run, that I couldn't win.
_Well, together, we pulled off the impossible._
**Now, I'm running for Governor of Virginia in the biggest election yet of Trump's second term.** National Republicans are throwing millions of dollars at us, and Trump himself has started launching attacks, but I know this team will have my back with so much on the line.
It may sound small, but your $5 contribution would help us make sure we can counter all the right-wing disinformation and continue sharing our agenda with voters.
**[So, what do you say? Will you contribute $5 (or even more!) right now to my campaign and Deb Haaland so we raise the resources we need this week? Every dollar adds up and allows us to fund ads, organizers, yard signs, and other critical investments.](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/807?c0=em_ms_dh_20251018_1_spanberger.Hx0FJ-&amount=5&refcode=em20251018-5129&t=3&akid=5129%2E400544%2E6XWCWO)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/807?c0=em_ms_dh_20251018_1_spanberger.Hx0FJ-&amount=5&refcode=em20251018-5129&t=22&akid=5129%2E400544%2E6XWCWO)**
Thank you so much,
Abigail Spanberger

clean_body: This won't be an easy race, but I'm used to overcoming the odds. Hi, it's Abigail Spanberger, Democratic nominee for Governor of Virginia. I'm respectfully asking: As the only Democrat who can flip a Governor's seat blue this year, I want to set an example for the nation. That's why we've knocked on over 700,000 doors so far. It's why we've launched plans to make life more affordable and strengthen our schools. And it's why we've committed to building the largest grassroots campaign in our Commonwealth's history. This won't be an easy race, but I'm used to overcoming the odds. Back in 2018, I ran for Congress in a district that Republicans had controlled for 50 years. People told me that I shouldn't run, that I couldn't win. Well, together, we pulled off the impossible. Now, I'm running for Governor of Virginia in the biggest election yet of Trump's second term. National Republicans are throwing millions of dollars at us, and Trump himself has started launching attacks, but I know this team will have my back with so much on the line. It may sound small, but your $5 contribution would help us make sure we can counter all the right-wing disinformation and continue sharing our agenda with voters. Thank you so much, Abigail Spanberger

| | | Peter, Across Pennsylvania, people are stepping up to take back local government and put power where it belongs — with working people. Josh is running to ensure county resources go where they’re needed most: affordable housing, good jobs, and public services that work for everyone. **In Allegheny County,** we’re endorsing **Kathleen Madonna-Emmerling** for County Council (District 1) — a United Steelworkers member, Kathleen is a proven voice for fair wages, affordable housing, and workers’ rights. On the County Council, she’ll fight to make the government responsive and accountable to the people it serves. We’re also standing with **Brandon Faust** and **Christine Stofko** for the Nazareth Area School Board, and **Mekkai Williams** and **Kait Linton** for the Hempfield School Board — candidates who believe public education should bring communities together, not tear them apart. They’re fighting for inclusive, well-resourced schools, respect for educators, and classrooms where every student feels safe, supported, and seen. **And in Columbia Borough in Lancaster County,** we’re proud to endorse **Jasmine Preston** for Mayor, along with **Kyle Ricker** , **Ethan Byers** , and **Jeanne Cooper** for Borough Council. United by a shared vision for a fairer, more welcoming Columbia, they’re focused on tackling housing costs, strengthening local businesses, and building a community where every voice matters. **These candidates share a common goal: putting power back in the hands of the people who live here. But they can’t do it alone.** If we want a government that truly works for working people — in every school district, borough, and county — we have to build it together. And of course, don’t forget to VOTE YES on the Supreme Court Judges! 👉 **[Chip in now to power these campaigns and help us reach every voter who needs to hear their message.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqt1KgGElNLHWwWbO_pXXNSCkdf3npZCjWZ-D7QYtnlPNiEcpyXBMD52sazA8qwo6VywDi7OkXU6TBBZCOsgvz3DNp5Sh5Si4yS-qWFxhYGtoQ6X4cC6lebXHwM-qcU3xECqfmy7GQT7YGiFHx8069yYoCZj5dYkgKaaLmCIMhG_qiVyktR25neKKjBWdORNbevu5eXazr9Tnji5qSYlede-nDXgvyIR3u0HbmblR1kOsMunVAN5j_mUVSaw3Ipq3WZAR8_Vi1XzF5NmO7klSC-ZGMagIeDZfzpDWJzLqP_0PWI8kJta0lwn84r_cQDQetGV4UhFCwhjynmByq9CfgT_5NdA8yCWsoYWjkZzHOY4rQ/4ku/zI23UyQUSHS9ZZ5pZN1Blw/h3/h001.k_885DIr90cT4wcazG3ARXt_77kG_UPlEFOlU9_LhdY)** **[Donate](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqt1KgGElNLHWwWbO_pXXNSCkdf3npZCjWZ-D7QYtnlPNiEcpyXBMD52sazA8qwo6VywDi7OkXU6TBBZCOsgvz3DYZzMdB2I2JPWTy54A1p6lExIgFObgGbSgofnG8OIiT3VipBDWC5giW6t9vIE7Gsae42Rj_ooqua80WMbUTVEk1_MQYEc9jRR5Swt5JPZ02wybiQOScYG9IhvNdjlcCZsudij9O0Iqz-hboAqSq83HG3CtGlkbnZ33M4xbJ0_zwR83Ori7ZPmr4EamS2MVElof0N7u6NuU3jQ31_9gcoDqAMbpu4wKUSdkOJyIA9s8PNtFzqJV4L24K_jMD0dCj9BlPLYd0nHU3QguI0-nPcsiA/4ku/zI23UyQUSHS9ZZ5pZN1Blw/h4/h001.gFpM1nwOCODaFXzDB-Tk9NkZCkwVt2PQE0XA-w_y18U)** When working families organize, we win — in our counties, our schools, and our towns. _Democrats and Radical Judges are trying to shut down all the progress we’ve made TOGETHER._

Thank you,

In less than nine months, Donald Trump and his cronies have enacted nearly _half_ of the Project 2025 agenda. **And f****riend****, it 's already eroding our democracy as designed.** From firing tens of _thousands_ of federal workers and replacing them with those loyal to Donald, to weaponizing the Justice Department against critics while protecting him and his allies, to gutting protections for our environment, our health, our civil rights, and so much more, the Trump regime has made clear that they'll answer to no one but Donald and the billionaires bankrolling his chaos. **This is fascism, plain and simple. But with your help, we can start building our firewall in state legislatures across the country in order to block his agenda ahead of the 2026 midterms.** [**With key races in Virginia, New Jersey, Louisiana, and Mississippi this November, the Democracy Defense Fund is counting on you to help shore up our fight and build a powerful anti-Trump regime bulwark across the states. body:
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117540016/576817212/-1473124489?amount=25&refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-10-18_ZOL_LA_ES-33691&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82OTYwNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmOGNhNDE0My00Y2FjLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N6bFYU6HAhG3RvFAvBVeB0_jH91OfzuyGijx0ocVk7A=)

| | |
---
Peter: Tomorrow I am kicking off my re-election campaign for Congress. This is a very challenging time for our country -- and, in this moment, each of us must do our part to defend our democracy if we hope to keep it. For me -- when the promise of our Constitution seems most tenuous -- that fight continues in Congress. I hope I can count on your continued encouragement and support in this effort.

As part of that, I’m spending most of today raising the resources that I’ll need to fund my campaign and win again. I would be very grateful for your help once more... and I promise will be put to good use. _**[Here’s the link for whatever you can do](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117540017/576817216/-1473124489?amount=25&refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-10-18_ZOL_LA_ES-33691&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82OTYwNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmOGNhNDE0My00Y2FjLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N6bFYU6HAhG3RvFAvBVeB0_jH91OfzuyGijx0ocVk7A=)**_.

As I kick off this next campaign, I want to reassure you that our democracy is not a fragile flower -- but it does need cultivating. It was built by dedicated work and it has been protected time and time again by people who love this country. That task and responsibility is now ours. I have no doubt that, together, we will continue to prove that its promise is worth preserving.

Thanks for giving this some thought and for all that you do.

Zoe

[_**PS: Tomorrow, Adam Kinzinger, House Democratic Caucus Chair Pete Aguilar, and Assembly Speaker Robert Rivas are joining us for my family BBQ and campaign kick-off. If you're in the area, please stop by! Here's the info.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117540018/576817218/-140053471?refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-10-18_ZOL_LA_ES-33691&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82OTYwNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmOGNhNDE0My00Y2FjLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N6bFYU6HAhG3RvFAvBVeB0_jH91OfzuyGijx0ocVk7A=)

name: Lindsey Scholten
subject: Re: What the federal government shutdown means for Oregon
body:
Hi Peter,
In case you missed it, I wanted to make sure you saw my update. Trump has cast a dark shadow over Oregon, and this situation is dire, but we can do this together. We need your support [https://secure.everyaction.com/rbY6SfJn4EmZpm6v2q-QSw2] to continue applying pressure on our elected leaders to protect our environment and health, and bring relief to Oregonians. It’s up to us to make sure Oregon leads on climate, that our elected officials uphold our values, and keep our beloved state the home that it is.
We can’t do this without you, Peter . [https://secure.everyaction.com/rbY6SfJn4EmZpm6v2q-QSw2?am=20&contactdata=XK9qQRMSZTKctP/wzkDvcIcDBbppfXq2HearOjMrZRIMgvWk4pA9mMHVFCHlAjJz0h1e+a8UcnhDOOcQCskeT/FSmheZsWRQesgkvvBWPshBH7VU/9/B0K5pLyethmes]
Give now [https://secure.everyaction.com/rbY6SfJn4EmZpm6v2q-QSw2?am=20&contactdata=XK9qQRMSZTKctP/wzkDvcIcDBbppfXq2HearOjMrZRIMgvWk4pA9mMHVFCHlAjJz0h1e+a8UcnhDOOcQCskeT/FSmheZsWRQesgkvvBWPshBH7VU/9/B0K5pLyethmes]
Will you renew your membership or make a special gift of $ 20 now to keep Oregon home? [https://secure.everyaction.com/rbY6SfJn4EmZpm6v2q-QSw2?am=20&contactdata=XK9qQRMSZTKctP/wzkDvcIcDBbppfXq2HearOjMrZRIMgvWk4pA9mMHVFCHlAjJz0h1e+a8UcnhDOOcQCskeT/FSmheZsWRQesgkvvBWPshBH7VU/9/B0K5pLyethmes]
In solidarity,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AVLCV/1/70877/images/Lindsey.png]
Lindsey Scholten (she/her)
OLCV Executive Director
P.S.: You can also make a free donation of $50 or $100 to OLCV PAC using Oregon’s Political Tax Credit. Click here to see if you qualify [https://secure.everyaction.com/2aYH7oLMDUGCzowlqvcGrQ2] !
To opt-out of future fundraising emails, please use this link here [https://secure.everyaction.com/p/24dczWT--Uqtcu_3MxYYog2] to log-in and update your email preferences. You will still receive other OLCV emails.
https://secure.everyaction.com/rbY6SfJn4EmZpm6v2q-QSw2?am=20&contactdata=XK9qQRMSZTKctP/wzkDvcIcDBbppfXq2HearOjMrZRIMgvWk4pA9mMHVFCHlAjJz0h1e+a8UcnhDOOcQCskeT/FSmheZsWRQesgkvvBWPshBH7VU/9/B0K5pLyethmes [https://secure.everyaction.com/rbY6SfJn4EmZpm6v2q-QSw2?am=20&contactdata=XK9qQRMSZTKctP/wzkDvcIcDBbppfXq2HearOjMrZRIMgvWk4pA9mMHVFCHlAjJz0h1e+a8UcnhDOOcQCskeT/FSmheZsWRQesgkvvBWPshBH7VU/9/B0K5pLyethmes]
Dear Peter,
Over a week ago, Trump and his allies shut down the federal government, severely impacting our communities and environment. This government shutdown means higher costs, less access to life-saving healthcare, unattended and unprotected public lands, and people out of work. It provides Republicans with the perfect opportunity to cut clean energy projects and jobs, allow the oil and gas industry to take over our public lands, and pursue Trump’s energy agenda that dirties our air and increases utility costs.
During the shutdown, the Trump Administration has already proposed another major funding cut for clean energy projects all in blue states, losing Oregon over $400 million for these very projects ( read more [https://www.nytimes.com/2025/10/02/climate/doe-clean-energy-programs-shutdown.html?unlocked_article_code=1.qU8.AD_Q.vwEsrzDOPhpI&smid=url-share] ).
We need your help, Peter. The consequences of this federal government shut down could be severe if our state does not take action. Will you make a generous contribution of $ 20 now to keep Oregon home? [https://secure.everyaction.com/rbY6SfJn4EmZpm6v2q-QSw2?am=20&contactdata=XK9qQRMSZTKctP/wzkDvcIcDBbppfXq2HearOjMrZRIMgvWk4pA9mMHVFCHlAjJz0h1e+a8UcnhDOOcQCskeT/FSmheZsWRQesgkvvBWPshBH7VU/9/B0K5pLyethmes]
Renew your membership [https://secure.everyaction.com/rbY6SfJn4EmZpm6v2q-QSw2?am=20&contactdata=XK9qQRMSZTKctP/wzkDvcIcDBbppfXq2HearOjMrZRIMgvWk4pA9mMHVFCHlAjJz0h1e+a8UcnhDOOcQCskeT/FSmheZsWRQesgkvvBWPshBH7VU/9/B0K5pLyethmes]
Our work here in Oregon is more important than ever. Just last week, Governor Tina Kotek signed an Executive Order to speed up the permitting process for clean energy projects, ensuring that they will be underway before the tax credit deadline next year — another Trump administration attack.
Now is the moment for our state lawmakers to take action. Oregon can stand up to Trump and his anti-climate agenda. We have already taken our first steps.
It’s crucial that our state lawmakers take action, but we need your support [https://secure.everyaction.com/rbY6SfJn4EmZpm6v2q-QSw2] to continue applying pressure on them to protect our environment and health, and bring relief to Oregonians. Oregon must be a leader on climate, and an example to the rest of the country of what climate action looks like.

clean_body: Hi Peter, In case you missed it, I wanted to make sure you saw my update. Trump has cast a dark shadow over Oregon, and this situation is dire, but we can do this together. We need your support [] to continue applying pressure on our elected leaders to protect our environment and health, and bring relief to Oregonians. It’s up to us to make sure Oregon leads on climate, that our elected officials uphold our values, and keep our beloved state the home that it is. We can’t do this without you, Peter . [] Give now [] Will you renew your membership or make a special gift of $ 20 now to keep Oregon home? [] In solidarity, [] Lindsey Scholten (she/her) OLCV Executive Director P.S.: You can also make a free donation of $50 or $100 to OLCV PAC using Oregon’s Political Tax Credit. Click here to see if you qualify [] ! To opt-out of future fundraising emails, please use this link here [] to log-in and update your email preferences. You will still receive other OLCV emails. [] Dear Peter, Over a week ago, Trump and his allies shut down the federal government, severely impacting our communities and environment. This government shutdown means higher costs, less access to life-saving healthcare, unattended and unprotected public lands, and people out of work. It provides Republicans with the perfect opportunity to cut clean energy projects and jobs, allow the oil and gas industry to take over our public lands, and pursue Trump’s energy agenda that dirties our air and increases utility costs. During the shutdown, the Trump Administration has already proposed another major funding cut for clean energy projects all in blue states, losing Oregon over $400 million for these very projects ( read more [] ). We need your help, Peter. The consequences of this federal government shut down could be severe if our state does not take action. Will you make a generous contribution of $ 20 now to keep Oregon home? [] Renew your membership [] Our work here in Oregon is more important than ever. Just last week, Governor Tina Kotek signed an Executive Order to speed up the permitting process for clean energy projects, ensuring that they will be underway before the tax credit deadline next year — another Trump administration attack. Now is the moment for our state lawmakers to take action. Oregon can stand up to Trump and his anti-climate agenda. We have already taken our first steps. It’s crucial that our state lawmakers take action, but we need your support [] to continue applying pressure on them to protect our environment and health, and bring relief to Oregonians. Oregon must be a leader on climate, and an example to the rest of the country of what climate action looks like. Please, renew your membership and make your impact in this movement with a generous gift of $20 now. [] Renew your membership [] Join us to work together to keep Oregon the home we know and love, ensuring we stick to the commitments we value. The situation is dire, and now is the time to give and take a stand for Oregon. This will take all of us, but if we each contribute what we can, there’s so much we can do for our home. Our work begins with you . Thank you for making it possible. name: Derek Tran
subject: Stop the Trump power grab (Prop 50)
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117540328/576818310/-1516755731?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTAxYmQyNzUtNGNhYy1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZWQ4YmE0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=pU8hrOUC6xALzZjYvAPamRYSlG5e68bXejJysyi-SZg=)
| Our country is at a crossroads.

I’ve always believed that democracy works best when every voter has an equal voice – no matter where they live, who they are, or how much money they make.

Now, California voters have a choice: Proposition 50 gives us the chance to fight back by protecting fair representation and our democracy while Republicans in Texas and other GOP-led states rig the game to keep the MAGA agenda in power.

**This is about standing up for every voice, and defending the democracy so many of us have fought to protect. And this November, we have a golden opportunity to level the playing field – not back down.**

She’s worked with both parties to secure investments in our communities and has been a true friend of veterans, seniors, first responders and working people. She’s a voice for us all in the People’s House. | = | = ---|--- Follow Marcy | | ---|--- Checks may also be mailed to: Kaptur for Congress - PO Box 899, Toledo, OH 43697 Paid for by Kaptur for Congress Email is a critical way to stay in touch with our supporters, but if you wish to unsubscribe, you may do so . body:
I’m out of options...
| **__A Note from Rep. Elise Stefanik__**
---
Friend,
---
Kathy Hochul is raising millions of dollars to BUY New York.
---
**Meanwhile, we’re struggling to raise the final $18,020 we need to reach our critical fundraising benchmark with only hours to go until the mid-month cutoff at 11:59 P.M. TONIGHT! **
---
At this late hour, I’m out of options. We don’t have any campaign checks left to cash, or any emergency funds we can draw from...
---
...My only hope of reaching our goal is this one final note to all of my _TOP_ grassroots supporters.
---
_As always: If you’ve given all that you can, or can’t afford to contribute right now, please simply disregard what I’m about to ask._
---
But if you can spare even just $5 to help us reach this goal, I could urgently use the support.

clean_body: I’m out of options... | A Note from Rep. Elise Stefanik Friend, Kathy Hochul is raising millions of dollars to BUY New York. Meanwhile, we’re struggling to raise the final $18,020 we need to reach our critical fundraising benchmark with only hours to go until the mid-month cutoff at 11:59 P.M. TONIGHT! At this late hour, I’m out of options. We don’t have any campaign checks left to cash, or any emergency funds we can draw from... ...My only hope of reaching our goal is this one final note to all of my TOP grassroots supporters. As always: If you’ve given all that you can, or can’t afford to contribute right now, please simply disregard what I’m about to ask. But if you can spare even just $5 to help us reach this goal, I could urgently use the support. Thank you, Rep. Elise Stefanik Chairwoman of House Republican Leadership Paid for by Team Elise, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Elise for Congress and E-PAC. www.elisestefanik.com PO Box 500 Glens Falls, NY 12801
